content:
Hello,

I'm not sure where to signal this...

I noticed the compiler-explorer link over on rust-for-linux.com is slightly wrong, it complains of "error: Option 'edition' given more than once" when you land on it.
It looks like the overrides button is also being activated which sets an edition.

I've generated a link which works a bit better: https://godbolt.org/z/d517nn8M6.

Let me know if there's a better place to report this.

Thanks

Thanks for spotting that and reporting it!

I have updated the link (I also updated the compiler version while at it) and reported the issue upstream (https://github.com/compiler-explorer/compiler-explorer/issues/5429). I noted you reported it -- if you let me know your GitHub handle, I will Cc you there too.

I think it is due to a recent change in CE that added the latest edition by default via the new Compiler Overrides feature. It may change again in the future since the CE team is considering making it more explicit, e.g. via the pre-filling idea that I requested originally, so I noted that we may need to update the link again if so.

Cool thanks!
My github handle is shadowmitia

I did see vaguely that CE default to an edition of rust. Never crossed my mind this would be a bug though :sweat_smile:
